headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: GRAINY COUNTERFEIT-SURVEILLANCE FRONT ROLLS ACROSS THE FACEBOOK DELTA AS FABRICATED CCTV FOOTAGE DOCUMENTS INCIDENTS THAT WERE NEVER RECORDED BY CAMERAS THAT WERE NEVER INSTALLED
advisory: A dense band of low-resolution 'security camera' content has moved into the lower feed corridors, depositing timestamped footage of impossible events across multiple platforms. Residents are advised that the graininess is a feature, not a defect; artificial noise is being applied to synthetic clips to imitate the authority of a real lens. The Service notes that a camera bolted to a corner of a hallway that does not exist cannot, by definition, have witnessed anything. Verify before you share; the timestamp is decorative.

regional_outlook.conditions: Persistent counterfeit-surveillance showers throughout the day, heaviest over the community-group channels. Expect footage of a moose entering a gas station, captioned as if it happened three towns over from you specifically.
Gusty repost activity carrying fake-CCTV clips at high velocity. Visibility poor; the same 11-second clip is arriving from forty accounts claiming forty different cities.
Compilation systems bundling synthetic surveillance into 'CAUGHT ON CAMERA' anthologies. Scattered real footage possible but statistically outnumbered.
Standing pools of auto-generated 'is this security footage real?' articles, each answering the question with three paragraphs and no conclusion.
A rash of new 'add realistic CCTV timestamp' utilities cresting the charts. Warm, opportunistic, and light on privacy policy.

specimen.status: Explosive summer bloom; range expanding rapidly
specimen.habitat: Community groups, repost swarms, and any comment section containing the phrase 'this can't be real'
specimen.field_marks: Deliberate low resolution and heavy grain; a plausible but unverifiable timestamp burned into the corner; a fixed high-angle 'security' vantage; and an event just implausible enough to share but just mundane enough to believe. Motion often stutters at the edges of moving objects.
specimen.call: CAUGHT ON CAMERA at 3:47 AM — you won't believe what walked into frame next
specimen.confusion_species: Genuine security footage, which is usually boring, poorly lit, and going nowhere. If the footage has a satisfying beginning, middle, and payoff, it was authored, not recorded — real cameras have no sense of narrative.

spotter_tip: Ask what the camera was doing there. Real surveillance is mounted for a dull reason and points at a dull place; if the angle is cinematically perfect for the event that unfolds, the lens is imaginary.
tomorrow: Counterfeit-surveillance front lingers overnight before shifting toward the Search Results Basin, where fact-check articles about it will be generated by the same weather system.

confirmed_sighting.report: Media-intelligence firm Meltwater reports that online mentions of 'AI slop' rose ninefold in 2025 over 2024, with negative sentiment peaking near 54 percent in October — confirming what our instruments have logged all year: the public can now see the front, and does not care for it.
